growth_records.cite: Chen et al. Microbial iron oxide respiration coupled to sulfide oxidation. Nature (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41586-025-09467-0. [PMC12545173]
growth_records.snippet: The calculated specific growth rate was higher in ferrihydrite cultures with dissolved sulfide (0.288 ± 0.015 day-1 at 1 mM ... )
growth_records.notes: Growth rate 0.288 +/-0.015 day-1 explicitly reported (specific growth rate k estimated via linear regression of ln(Cell_t/Cell0) vs time over exponential phase, flow cytometry cell counts); converted to h-1 by dividing by 24 (0.288/24=0.0120 h-1). Culture: ferrihydrite + daily 1 mM sulfide addition, anaerobic chamber, room temperature (not given as a number), alkaline medium pH 9.3. Sulfate secretion rates from same 1 mM sulfide + ferrihydrite condition, higher time resolution sub-experiment: phase I (first 70 min, sulfide present) 0.69 fmol cell-1 h-1; phase II (after sulfide depletion) 0.061 fmol cell-1 h-1, both reported directly. [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature]

growth_records.agit: static (serum/Schott bottle incubations; no shaking reported)
growth_records.cite: Chen et al. Microbial iron oxide respiration coupled to sulfide oxidation. Nature (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41586-025-09467-0. [PMC12545173]
growth_records.snippet: ...(0.436 ± 0.074 day-1 at 50 µM) compared to those with solid phase FeS (0.089 ± 0.005 day-1), probably owing to the limited solubility of FeS.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate 0.436 +/-0.074 day-1 explicitly reported (linear regression of ln(Cell_t/Cell0) vs time, flow cytometry); converted to h-1 = 0.436/24 = 0.01817. Culture: ferrihydrite + periodic ~50 uM sulfide addition, anaerobic, room temperature, alkaline medium pH 9.3. [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature]
